name: Aggregate Capacity Warning
description: Detects aggregates with used capacity between 85% and 95%. High utilization can lead to performance degradation and risk of running out of space.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 AS used_pct FROM ontap_aggregates WHERE system_id = ? AND CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 > 85 AND CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 <= 95
remediation_template: Aggregate '{{name}}' is at {{used_pct | round(1)}}% capacity. Consider adding disks, enabling storage tiering, or moving volumes to less-utilized aggregates.

name: Aggregate Capacity Critical
description: Detects aggregates with used capacity exceeding 95%. Critically full aggregates can cause write failures and data unavailability.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 AS used_pct FROM ontap_aggregates WHERE system_id = ? AND CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 > 95
remediation_template: Aggregate '{{name}}' is critically full at {{used_pct | round(1)}}% capacity. Immediately free space by deleting unnecessary snapshots, moving volumes, or adding disks.

name: Volume Capacity Warning
description: Detects volumes with used capacity between 85% and 95%. Approaching full volumes may cause application disruptions.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 AS used_pct FROM ontap_volumes WHERE system_id = ? AND CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 > 85 AND CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 <= 95
remediation_template: Volume '{{name}}' is at {{used_pct | round(1)}}% capacity. Consider enabling autogrow, increasing volume size, or archiving data.

name: Volume Capacity Critical
description: Detects volumes with used capacity exceeding 95%. Critically full volumes can cause data loss and application outages.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 AS used_pct FROM ontap_volumes WHERE system_id = ? AND CAST(used_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 > 95
remediation_template: Volume '{{name}}' is critically full at {{used_pct | round(1)}}% capacity. Immediately increase volume size, enable autogrow, or free space to prevent data loss.

name: LIF Not on Home Port
description: Detects logical interfaces (LIFs) that have migrated away from their home node or port. LIFs not on home can cause suboptimal data paths and performance issues.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, home_node, home_port, current_node, current_port FROM ontap_lifs WHERE system_id = ? AND (home_node != current_node OR home_port != current_port)
remediation_template: LIF '{{name}}' has migrated from home ({{home_node}}:{{home_port}}) to ({{current_node}}:{{current_port}}). Revert the LIF to its home port using 'network interface revert' or investigate why failover occurred.

name: Node Not Healthy
description: Detects ONTAP nodes reporting an unhealthy status. An unhealthy node may indicate hardware failures, HA partner issues, or critical subsystem errors.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name FROM ontap_nodes WHERE system_id = ? AND is_healthy = 0
remediation_template: Node '{{name}}' is reporting unhealthy status. Check the node's hardware health, HA partner connectivity, and EMS logs for root cause. Consider initiating a takeover if the partner is functional.

name: SnapMirror Relationship Unhealthy
description: Detects SnapMirror relationships that are not in a healthy state. Unhealthy relationships mean replication is not functioning and data protection is compromised.
check_query: SELECT system_id, source_path, destination_path, state FROM ontap_snapmirror WHERE system_id = ? AND healthy = 0
remediation_template: SnapMirror relationship from '{{source_path}}' to '{{destination_path}}' is unhealthy (state: {{state}}). Resynchronize or re-establish the relationship to restore data protection.

name: SnapMirror Lag Exceeds 24 Hours
description: Detects SnapMirror relationships with a lag time exceeding 24 hours (86400 seconds). Excessive lag means the disaster recovery copy is significantly behind the source.
check_query: SELECT system_id, source_path, destination_path, lag_time, state FROM ontap_snapmirror WHERE system_id = ? AND lag_time > 86400
remediation_template: SnapMirror relationship '{{source_path}}' -> '{{destination_path}}' has a lag of {{lag_time}} seconds. Investigate network bandwidth, schedule frequency, and transfer performance. Trigger a manual update if needed.

name: Critical EMS Events Detected
description: Detects critical or emergency EMS (Event Management System) events in the last 24 hours that require immediate attention.
check_query: SELECT system_id, severity, message_name, time FROM ontap_ems_events WHERE system_id = ? AND severity IN ('critical', 'emergency', 'alert') AND time >= datetime('now', '-24 hours')
remediation_template: Critical EMS event '{{message_name}}' (severity: {{severity}}) detected at {{time}}. Review the event details and take corrective action per NetApp KB recommendations.

name: Snapshot Reserve Overflow
description: Detects volumes where snapshot consumption has exceeded the configured snapshot reserve, spilling into the active filesystem space.
check_query: SELECT v.system_id, v.name, v.snapshot_reserve_pct, CAST(SUM(s.size_bytes) AS REAL) / NULLIF(v.size_bytes, 0) * 100 AS snap_used_pct FROM ontap_volumes v JOIN ontap_snapshots s ON v.system_id = s.system_id AND v.name = s.volume_id WHERE v.system_id = ? GROUP BY v.system_id, v.name HAVING snap_used_pct > v.snapshot_reserve_pct
remediation_template: Volume '{{name}}' snapshot usage ({{snap_used_pct | round(1)}}%) exceeds the configured reserve ({{snapshot_reserve_pct}}%). Delete unnecessary snapshots or increase the snapshot reserve percentage.

name: Cluster Health Degraded
description: Detects ONTAP clusters not reporting a healthy cluster_health status. A degraded cluster may have subsystem failures or quorum issues.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, version, cluster_health FROM ontap_clusters WHERE system_id = ? AND cluster_health != 'true' AND cluster_health != 'ok'
remediation_template: Cluster '{{name}}' health is '{{cluster_health}}'. Run 'system health status show' and 'cluster ring show' to identify the degraded subsystems and take corrective action.

name: Volume Offline
description: Detects ONTAP volumes that are in an offline state. Offline volumes are inaccessible to clients and applications.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, state FROM ontap_volumes WHERE system_id = ? AND state = 'offline'
remediation_template: Volume '{{name}}' is offline. Bring it online using 'volume online -vserver <svm> -volume {{name}}' after verifying the aggregate is available and there are no underlying issues.

name: Aggregate Offline
description: Detects ONTAP aggregates that are not in an online state. Offline aggregates make all contained volumes unavailable.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, state FROM ontap_aggregates WHERE system_id = ? AND state != 'online'
remediation_template: Aggregate '{{name}}' is in state '{{state}}'. Investigate disk failures, RAID group status, and attempt to bring the aggregate online. Contact NetApp Support if the aggregate cannot be recovered.

name: Failed Disks Detected
description: Detects disks in a failed, broken, or unknown state. Failed disks reduce RAID protection and must be replaced promptly.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, state, container_type FROM ontap_disks WHERE system_id = ? AND state IN ('failed', 'broken', 'unknown')
remediation_template: Disk '{{name}}' is in '{{state}}' state. Replace the failed disk immediately. ONTAP should automatically reconstruct the RAID group using a spare disk if available.

name: Low Spare Disk Count
description: Checks for nodes with fewer than 2 spare disks. Insufficient spares mean a disk failure cannot be automatically reconstructed, putting data at risk.
check_query: SELECT system_id, COUNT(*) AS spare_count FROM ontap_disks WHERE system_id = ? AND container_type = 'spare' GROUP BY system_id HAVING spare_count < 2
remediation_template: System has only {{spare_count}} spare disk(s). NetApp best practice recommends at least 2 hot spares per node. Add spare disks to maintain RAID protection.

name: FIPS 140-2 Compliance Not Enabled
description: Checks if FIPS 140-2 compliance mode is not enabled on the cluster. FIPS ensures cryptographic modules meet federal security standards.
check_query: SELECT system_id, fips_enabled FROM ontap_security WHERE system_id = ? AND fips_enabled = 0
remediation_template: FIPS 140-2 compliance is not enabled. Enable it using 'security config modify -interface SSL -is-fips-enabled true'. Note: this requires a reboot of management interfaces.

name: Volumes Without Encryption (ARP Risk)
description: Detects data volumes that do not have encryption enabled. Unencrypted volumes are vulnerable to data theft if physical media is compromised and lack Anti-Ransomware Protection (ARP) capabilities.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, type FROM ontap_volumes WHERE system_id = ? AND is_encrypted = 0 AND type = 'rw'
remediation_template: Volume '{{name}}' is not encrypted. Enable NetApp Volume Encryption (NVE) or NetApp Aggregate Encryption (NAE) and configure Anti-Ransomware Protection (ARP) for enhanced security.

name: Volumes Without QoS Policy
description: Detects systems with no QoS policies configured. Without QoS, a single workload can monopolize cluster resources and impact other workloads.
check_query: SELECT ? AS system_id WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ontap_qos_policies WHERE system_id = ?)
remediation_template: No QoS policies are configured on this system. Define adaptive or fixed QoS policy groups and assign them to volumes to prevent workload contention.

name: LIF Operationally Down
description: Detects logical interfaces that are operationally down, meaning clients cannot access data through these network paths.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, home_node, home_port, operational_status FROM ontap_lifs WHERE system_id = ? AND operational_status = 'down'
remediation_template: LIF '{{name}}' on {{home_node}}:{{home_port}} is operationally down. Check the physical port status, VLAN configuration, and network connectivity. Use 'network interface modify' to bring it up.

name: License Expiring Soon
description: Detects ONTAP feature licenses that are expiring within the next 90 days. Expired licenses may disable critical features.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, state, expiry_date FROM ontap_licenses WHERE system_id = ? AND expiry_date IS NOT NULL AND expiry_date != '' AND expiry_date <= date('now', '+90 days') AND expiry_date > date('now')
remediation_template: License '{{name}}' expires on {{expiry_date}}. Contact your NetApp sales representative or use the NetApp License Manager to renew the license before it expires.

name: License Expired
description: Detects ONTAP feature licenses that have already expired. Expired licenses may cause features to stop functioning.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, state, expiry_date FROM ontap_licenses WHERE system_id = ? AND expiry_date IS NOT NULL AND expiry_date != '' AND expiry_date <= date('now')
remediation_template: License '{{name}}' expired on {{expiry_date}}. Immediately renew the license to restore full functionality. Contact NetApp support for emergency license keys.

name: Volumes Without FabricPool Tiering
description: Detects read-write volumes that do not have a FabricPool tiering policy configured. Tiering cold data to object storage can significantly reduce storage costs.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, tiering_policy FROM ontap_volumes WHERE system_id = ? AND type = 'rw' AND (tiering_policy IS NULL OR tiering_policy = 'none')
remediation_template: Volume '{{name}}' has no FabricPool tiering policy. Consider enabling 'auto' or 'snapshot-only' tiering to move cold data to lower-cost object storage.

name: Root Aggregate Low Space
description: Detects root aggregates with less than 15% free space. Root aggregates contain critical system volumes and running low can cause node instability.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, CAST(available_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 AS free_pct FROM ontap_aggregates WHERE system_id = ? AND is_root = 1 AND CAST(available_bytes AS REAL) / NULLIF(size_bytes, 0) * 100 < 15
remediation_template: Root aggregate '{{name}}' has only {{free_pct | round(1)}}% free space. Remove non-essential volumes from the root aggregate and ensure only the root volume and node management volumes reside on it.
